---
title: "Development of Spatial Targeting Tools for Togo"
---

```{=html}
<div style="display:flex; gap:0.6rem; flex-wrap:wrap; margin-bottom:1.8rem;">
  <span style="background:#002366; color:white; border-radius:6px; padding:0.3rem 0.8rem; font-size:0.8rem; font-weight:600;">Spatial Analysis</span>
  <span style="background:#e8eef8; color:#002366; border-radius:6px; padding:0.3rem 0.8rem; font-size:0.8rem; font-weight:600;">Public Policy</span>
  <span style="background:#e8eef8; color:#002366; border-radius:6px; padding:0.3rem 0.8rem; font-size:0.8rem; font-weight:600;">Remote Sensing</span>
  <span style="background:#e8eef8; color:#002366; border-radius:6px; padding:0.3rem 0.8rem; font-size:0.8rem; font-weight:600;">Togo · 2024</span>
</div>
```

## Introduction

Effective targeting of social and infrastructure investments requires more than aggregate poverty statistics — it demands a fine-grained understanding of *where* vulnerabilities concentrate, overlap, and interact across space. In contexts where administrative capacity is limited and resources are scarce, identifying priority territories is both a technical and a political challenge.

This project develops a **data-driven territorial targeting tool for Togo** designed to support evidence-based allocation of public investments. The tool integrates population and poverty information from the 2022 national census with a rich set of satellite-derived and geospatial indicators. These inputs are combined into a **composite priority index** that ranks all cantons nationwide and all neighborhoods (*quartiers*) in Greater Lomé according to the intensity of overlapping vulnerabilities.

The objective is operational: to provide policymakers, development agencies, and local governments with a transparent, reproducible framework for identifying areas where interventions are likely to generate the greatest social impact — moving beyond ad hoc targeting toward a structured, data-grounded approach.

---

## Methodology

```{=html}
<div style="background:#f5f8ff; border-left:4px solid #002366; border-radius:0 8px 8px 0; padding:1.2rem 1.5rem; margin-bottom:1.5rem;">
  <strong>Core principle:</strong> The index aggregates multiple vulnerability dimensions into a single ranked score, allowing direct comparison across territorial units at two scales — canton (national) and neighborhood (Greater Lomé).
</div>
```

The construction of the priority index follows four main steps:

**1. Unit of analysis**

The tool operates at two complementary scales:

- **Canton level** — 117 cantons nationwide, enabling national-level prioritization of rural and peri-urban territories.
- **Neighborhood (*quartier*) level** — fine-grained targeting within Greater Lomé, where spatial heterogeneity is high and aggregate statistics mask localized deprivation.

**2. Indicator normalization**

Each raw input is transformed into a normalized score on a common 0–1 scale using min-max normalization, so that higher values consistently indicate greater vulnerability or need. For indicators where higher raw values reflect *better* conditions (e.g., wealth index, road accessibility), the score is inverted.

**3. Composite index construction**

Normalized indicators are aggregated into a weighted composite score. Weights reflect the relative policy relevance of each dimension and are calibrated through stakeholder consultation and sensitivity analysis. The resulting index provides a continuous vulnerability score for each territorial unit.

**4. Ranking and classification**

Units are ranked from highest to lowest priority score and classified into quintiles for visualization and operational use. The top quintile identifies territories where multiple deprivations overlap — the primary targets for investment.

---

## Inputs

The index integrates **seven categories of indicators**, combining administrative, satellite, and geospatial data sources:

```{=html}
<div style="display:grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(260px, 1fr)); gap:1.2rem; margin:1.5rem 0;">

  <div style="border:1px solid #dde3f0; border-radius:9px; padding:1.2rem 1.4rem; background:#fff;">
    <div style="font-size:1.4rem; margin-bottom:0.5rem;">👥</div>
    <div style="font-weight:700; color:#002366; margin-bottom:0.4rem;">Population & Poverty</div>
    <div style="font-size:0.88rem; color:#555; line-height:1.55;">Population counts, poverty headcount ratios, and household welfare estimates from the 2022 RGPH census and the EHCVM household survey.</div>
  </div>

  <div style="border:1px solid #dde3f0; border-radius:9px; padding:1.2rem 1.4rem; background:#fff;">
    <div style="font-size:1.4rem; margin-bottom:0.5rem;">🌙</div>
    <div style="font-weight:700; color:#002366; margin-bottom:0.4rem;">Nighttime Lights</div>
    <div style="font-size:0.88rem; color:#555; line-height:1.55;">NASA VIIRS nighttime light radiance as a proxy for economic activity and electrification. Low luminosity signals infrastructure deprivation.</div>
  </div>

  <div style="border:1px solid #dde3f0; border-radius:9px; padding:1.2rem 1.4rem; background:#fff;">
    <div style="font-size:1.4rem; margin-bottom:0.5rem;">💰</div>
    <div style="font-weight:700; color:#002366; margin-bottom:0.4rem;">Relative Wealth Index</div>
    <div style="font-size:0.88rem; color:#555; line-height:1.55;">Meta / Facebook Relative Wealth Index — a high-resolution satellite-based estimate of relative asset wealth at 2.4 km grid level.</div>
  </div>

  <div style="border:1px solid #dde3f0; border-radius:9px; padding:1.2rem 1.4rem; background:#fff;">
    <div style="font-size:1.4rem; margin-bottom:0.5rem;">🏥</div>
    <div style="font-weight:700; color:#002366; margin-bottom:0.4rem;">Service Accessibility</div>
    <div style="font-size:0.88rem; color:#555; line-height:1.55;">Travel time to the nearest health facility, school, and market, computed from OpenStreetMap and road network data using friction surfaces.</div>
  </div>

  <div style="border:1px solid #dde3f0; border-radius:9px; padding:1.2rem 1.4rem; background:#fff;">
    <div style="font-size:1.4rem; margin-bottom:0.5rem;">🛣️</div>
    <div style="font-weight:700; color:#002366; margin-bottom:0.4rem;">Road Network</div>
    <div style="font-size:0.88rem; color:#555; line-height:1.55;">Road density and quality classification from OpenStreetMap, capturing physical connectivity and isolation of territorial units.</div>
  </div>

  <div style="border:1px solid #dde3f0; border-radius:9px; padding:1.2rem 1.4rem; background:#fff;">
    <div style="font-size:1.4rem; margin-bottom:0.5rem;">🌊</div>
    <div style="font-weight:700; color:#002366; margin-bottom:0.4rem;">Flood Risk</div>
    <div style="font-size:0.88rem; color:#555; line-height:1.55;">Flood hazard exposure derived from the Global Flood Database (GFD) and FATHOM hydrological models, identifying climate-vulnerable zones.</div>
  </div>

  <div style="border:1px solid #dde3f0; border-radius:9px; padding:1.2rem 1.4rem; background:#fff;">
    <div style="font-size:1.4rem; margin-bottom:0.5rem;">🏘️</div>
    <div style="font-weight:700; color:#002366; margin-bottom:0.4rem;">Built-up Density</div>
    <div style="font-size:0.88rem; color:#555; line-height:1.55;">Human settlement layer from the Global Human Settlement Layer (GHSL), measuring built-up area density as an indicator of urbanization pressure.</div>
  </div>

</div>
```

---

## Results

```{=html}
<div style="background:#f5f8ff; border:1px solid #dde3f0; border-radius:9px; padding:1.5rem 1.8rem; text-align:center; color:#555;">
  <div style="font-size:1.1rem; font-weight:600; color:#002366; margin-bottom:0.5rem;">Results coming soon</div>
  <div style="font-size:0.92rem; line-height:1.6;">
    This section will present canton-level and neighborhood-level priority maps, quintile distributions, and sensitivity analyses. Results are currently being finalized.
  </div>
</div>
```

---

<p style="font-size:0.85rem; color:#888;">← <a href="resources.html">Back to Ideas & Experiments</a></p>
